Title: Smallholders welcome hike in rubber replanting assistance rate
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government's move to increase the assistance rate under the Rubber Replanting Programme has been welcomed by smallholders facing challenges related to costs and uncertainty in commodity prices.
Checks by Bernama found that most of them expressed appreciation to the government for its continued commitment to safeguarding their welfare and strengthening the resilience of smallholders, while also helping to boost the country's rubber production.
In
PAHANG
, rubber smallholder Adnan Husin, 54, said the increase in assistance eases the burden on the group in carrying out replanting activities and improving yields in the future.
"Previously, smallholders might have had to postpone their plans to replant rubber due to the high costs involved.
"Now, they can proceed because they know the returns will be greater compared with tapping old rubber trees," he told Bernama.
Another smallholder, Husin Mohamad, 63, said the increase in assistance would help him avoid going into debt to carry out replanting on his smallholding.
"My rubber trees are mostly old, and their yield is low. I want to replant, but I still don't have enough capital.
"Thankfully, with the increase in government assistance, my plan to plant new rubber trees has been made easier," he said.
In
KEDAH
, Rodhi Md Rejab, 49, from Felda Laka Selatan in Jitra, described the increase in the assistance rate as a positive step in strengthening the country's rubber industry while easing the burden on smallholders.
"However, due attention should also be given to hired tappers who work on other people's smallholdings. This group is among those most affected by the rising cost of living, yet receives less attention in terms of assistance or specific support," he said.
For Mohd Zaki Othman, 56, from Padang Terap, who has been involved in rubber cultivation for over 15 years, the increase in the assistance rate would help ease cost pressures amid rising fertiliser and pesticide prices driven by global economic factors.
"The higher assistance will ensure that rubber tappers continue to work their smallholdings, as the rubber industry is becoming increasingly challenging… at the same time, it can help rubber tappers break free from the cycle of poverty," he said.
In
JOHOR
, National Association of Smallholders Malaysia (PKPKM) state chairman Khairul Aman Mohd Shah described the increase in the assistance rate as a positive development that could attract young people to venture into the commodity sector.
He said the effort is important to continue the legacy of rubber cultivation and to produce a new generation that can help sustain and enhance the country's production.
"We were once the world's leading rubber producer, so it is not impossible for us to relive that golden era," he said.
He said the assistance helps smallholders in various aspects, including land preparation as well as the supply of seedlings and fertilisers throughout the planting period, thereby boosting their motivation to continue their efforts.
At the same time, he opined that the government needs to strengthen the marketing aspect by ensuring more stable rubber prices to encourage increased productivity.
Yesterday, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the increase in the assistance rate for the Rubber Replanting Programme in Peninsular Malaysia and Sabah in the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) demonstrates the government's continued commitment to supporting rubber smallholders.
He said that through the improvements, the assistance rate in Peninsular Malaysia has been increased from RM13,710 per ha to RM20,000 per ha, while in Sabah it has been raised from RM14,955 per ha to RM23,000 per ha.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM15.5bil recovery an unmatched milestone in nation's history - Anwar
Article:
KOTA BARU: The government has recovered RM15.5 billion in funds and assets over recent years through firm anti-corruption enforcement, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Speaking at the Kelantan PKR Mini Convention here, Anwar, who is also PKR president, said the unprecedented recovery was the result of decisive action to curb leakages and corruption in the country.
"Two years ago, we collected RM15.5 billion from leakages because we were firm and took strict action to prevent abuse and corruption in our nation.
"This is part of the reform agenda implemented by the current government, led by PKR.
"While we are facing economic challenges, never in our history have we managed to reclaim such a large amount," he said.
Anwar stressed that the government would not tolerate abuse of power or the misuse of public funds.
"We will not allow anyone to abuse power or take what belongs to the people.
"Good governance is our priority, and no one will be protected if they are found to be in the wrong," he said.
Also present were PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh and Kelantan PKR chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Supardi Md Noor.
During the programme, Anwar also received applications from more than 400 new members.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Come contest in Kedah, says Sanusi to KJ
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Kedah is open to any political figure who wishes to contest in the state, including former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin, says Kedah Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor.
Sanusi said that in a democratic country, anyone was free to contest in any state during an election.
"Yes, anyone can come and contest in Kedah. Come and contest in Kedah — let's see what the people of Kedah say. Kedah is open to everyone.
"(Khairy contesting in Kedah) No problem. His mother is from Kedah. He understands Kedah culture.
"It's up to them. Kedah is an open field for everybody. Our country is democratic. Anyone can contest anywhere. I welcome whoever wants to contest in Kedah," he said after attending the 2026 Kedah diaspora Aidilfitri gathering here today.
The Kedah menteri besar was commenting on Umno's suggestion that a figure like Khairy was needed to revive the party's fortunes in the state.
Umno Youth information chief for Kedah Mohd Safwan Jaafar had previously been reported as saying that Barisan Nasional needed a figure like Khairy to reinvigorate its political revival, adding that the former health minister's ideology and thinking could help shift voter sentiment in Kedah.
Yesterday, former Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and Khairy were among 6,252 former leaders and members at various levels who were readmitted into the party. https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/04/1420300/im-home-says-kj-after-umno-readmission?fbclid=IwY2xjawRQIGZleHRuA2FlbQIxMABicmlkETEweEgwTUtNODJURUtiV2Zmc3J0YwZhcHBfaWQQMjIyMDM5MTc4ODIwMDg5MgABHnOzURgsnf1-M3tSXpjQFMn7ICPS92TPPztNr8LXZ55m8DUV_jWfoXC_b0fT_aem_i-HKwYmSBIy0KZSxMnVl5Q
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: App for Rahmah Madani sales info
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry has introduced the Info Jualan Rahmah Madani application (app) to provide the public with faster and more accurate information on the programme.
Deputy Minister Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said the app enables users to check locations, dates and programme details in real time, helping them plan daily spending more effectively.
She said the ministry had previously received frequent enquiries on Rahmah Sale locations and schedules, and the app was expected to address the issue.
"The app can be downloaded via smartphones and will make it easier for the public to obtain information quickly," she told reporters after attending the Sentuhan Kasih and Jualan Rahmah Madani programme at the PPR Kota Baru multipurpose hall here today.
At the same event, 50 recipients from the Kota Lama state constituency under the Kota Baru parliamentary area received Bakul Rahmah contributions.
In a related development, Fuziah said 7,320 series of the Jualan Rahmah Madani programme were held nationwide from Jan 1 to March 31 this year, reflecting the government's continued commitment to helping the people cope with rising living costs.
In Kelantan alone, 496 programmes were organised, recording 386,888 purchase transactions.
"This achievement proves the effectiveness of the initiative in ensuring continued access to basic necessities at affordable prices, in line with the government's aspiration that no group is left behind," she said.
She added that the government would continue to strengthen and expand the programme in a more strategic and targeted manner, taking into account global economic challenges, including rising logistics costs and supply chain pressures.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teacher arrested for allegedly molesting special needs student in Jasin
Article:
JASIN: A teacher has been arrested for allegedly molesting a 15-year-old special needs student at a secondary school here last Thursday.
The incident was revealed after the Form Three girl, who has learning disabilities, refused to attend school and eventually confided in her elder sister.
Sources said the victim was in the staffroom with two friends at 1pm on April 16 when the 60-year-old male teacher approached her.
The suspect allegedly molested the girl and questioned her regarding the physical development of her friends.
It is understood the teacher warned the victim not to report the matter, despite the incident being witnessed by her peers.
The victim and her family lodged a police report following the ordeal, which led to the teacher's arrest.
Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar confirmed the arrest and said the case is being investigated under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN to maintain seat distribution formula for next state, general elections - Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional (BN) will maintain the existing seat distribution formula to face the upcoming state elections and general election (GE), said its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
He said BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir would be tasked with examining the need to make adjustments or increase the number of seats for coalition partners, including 'Friends of BN'.
"We want to prove that our togetherness all this time can be translated into the form of seat distribution that is suitable for them," he said at a press conference after officiating the 46th Kimma Annual General Meeting at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur (WTCKL) today.
Also present were Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir, Kimma president Datuk Seri Syed Ibrahim Kader, senior leaders of component parties, friends of BN, as well as 938 delegates involving 95 divisions from across the country.
The deputy prime minister said the BN machinery needs to start actively attracting support from outside voters since support from component party members alone is not enough to guarantee a candidate's victory.
"We are aware that support from component party members alone is not enough to win the election. Therefore, the machinery needs to start attracting support from voters who are not members of any component party," he said.
Touching on MIC's position in the coalition, he stressed that the party remains a core BN party and has never officially expressed its intention to leave.
"Although there is a letter linked to Perikatan Nasional, MIC denies that they have ever applied to join the party," he said.
Commenting on Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan's suggestion that the Johor and Melaka state elections be postponed until the economy stabilises and the oil price volatility ends, Zahid stressed that the matter was just a personal view.
"That is his personal view and not the official stance of Umno or Barisan Nasional," he said.
Previously, Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri
Ab Rauf Yusoh
said the Melaka state government's term of office would end on Nov 30, 2026, to give way to the state's election.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan development allocation raised to RM8bil, says Anwar
Article:
KOTA BARU: Development allocation for Kelantan has been increased from RM5 billion to RM8 billion, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced today.
Speaking at the PKR mini convention here, Anwar, who is also PKR president, said the additional funding reflected the Madani government's commitment to addressing development gaps and improving the well-being of the people in the state.
"I have come to Kelantan many times, but this time I see a stronger determination among the people.
"As a government, we administer the country together with our coalition partners, and while there are differing views, our focus remains clear," he said.
He stressed that the government's approach was to assist all Malaysians in need, regardless of political affiliation.
"We do not ask which party you belong to. If you fall within the category that needs help, we will assist through the appropriate channels implemented by the Implementation Coordination Unit," he said.
Anwar cited several initiatives, including today's RM20 million allocation announced in Pasir Puteh, repairs to a collapsed bridge in Kuala Krai, and approved funding to rebuild parts of a school in Tumpat damaged by fire.
"This is what the younger generation must understand — PKR is a party that works and delivers.
"Our struggle is not merely rhetorical, but one that fulfils the aspirations of the people," he said.
He added that despite current economic uncertainties, the government remained committed to eradicating hardcore poverty and ensuring no group is left behind.
"Even if they are not our supporters or voters, it is still our responsibility.
"After more than 70 years of independence, there is no reason we cannot resolve these issues," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Firemen struggle to douse 20ha peat swamp fire in Dungun
Article:
DUNGUN: Firefighters are concentrating on buffer zones between a forest reserve and residential areas to control a peat swamp forest fire covering 20ha near Kampung Rhu Batil, Kuala Abang.
Dungun Fire and Rescue Station chief Deputy Fire Superintendent II Yahaya Hanafi said the strategy was taken to prevent the fire, which started last Saturday, from spreading to nearby areas.
"So far, 11ha of the total affected area have been successfully extinguished," he told reporters when met at the scene today.
He said 26 personnel, including members of the Malaysia Volunteers Corps Department (Rela) and the Civil Defence Force (CDF), were involved in the operation.
Yahaya said efforts to extinguish the fire were hindered by limited access and insufficient water sources at the site.
He said firefighters used water from nearby ditches and disused mines to fight the fire.
"Our personnel also had to dig a well at the site to secure an adequate water supply.
"The current hot and dry weather has also caused the fire to spread rapidly across the area," he said.
He said the operation is expected to continue for another week because peat swamp fires are difficult to fully extinguish due to underground embers.
The fire is believed to have started from uncontrolled open burning near the forest.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar approves RM500,000 aid for fire-hit Bachok religious school
Article:
PASIR PUTEH: The federal government has approved RM500,000 in immediate aid for Sekolah Menengah Ugama (A) Darul Ulum Al-Muhammadiah in Kampung Kolam, Bachok, following a fire on April 12.
Perdana Menteri Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the allocation is intended to repair and restore the school's facilities.
"The Madani government is saddened by the fire at SMU (A) Darul Ulum Al-Muhammadiah, which saw three blocks of school buildings destroyed or damaged.
"As such, the government will allocate RM500,000 to assist the school administration in repairing the infrastructure affected by the incident," he said during a speech at Masjid Mukim Gong Chapa Temila here today.
Also present were Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
Anwar said he decided on the aid after receiving a formal request from the menteri besar regarding the incident.
"The menteri besar thought I only looked after Kelantan, but I have many other states to care for. However, because he is a friend, I have approved this immediate aid of half a million ringgit," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: LTSIP runway extension ready by 2027, poised to unlock long-haul travel potential
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) runway extension project, which is currently underway, is expected to be completed by the end of 2027.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook said international flight operations do not need to wait until the runway is completed, as the existing 2,400m runway is already sufficient for flights to Asean countries such as Indonesia and Thailand, which typically take between two and three hours.
"However, once the runway is extended, it will be more attractive to airlines as aircrafts will have sufficient take-off distance, especially when carrying heavier fuel loads.
"This will enable longer-haul flights of about five to six hours, for example, to China or South Korea in the future," he said at a press conference at the new LTSIP terminal here today.
Loke said the ministry will continue discussions with local airlines such as Malaysia Airlines, Batik Air and AirAsia to increase flight frequencies, while also welcoming the entry of international carriers into LTSIP.
He said improved connectivity would attract more visitors to Kelantan and further boost the state's tourism sector.
Meanwhile, Loke said LTSIP is also seeing strong demand for domestic travel, particularly on the strategic Kota Baru-Kuala Lumpur route, which is among the busiest in the country.
"Currently, Malaysia Airlines, AirAsia, Firefly and Batik Air Malaysia operate flights to Kota Bharu, with a total of 125 weekly flights covering six key destinations, namely Kuala Lumpur, Kota Kinabalu, Johor Baru, Kuching, Penang and Subang," he said.
He added that this development has contributed to steady passenger traffic growth throughout the year.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KJ vows to help rebuild public confidence in Umno
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin says he returns to the party with no demands, but with a renewed commitment to serve, contribute and help rebuild public confidence in Umno.
"I return not with any demands, but with a determination to serve and contribute as best as I can to the strength of Umno.
"I am ready to cooperate with all levels of leadership and the grassroots in order to restore public confidence in the party," he said in a Facebook post today.
He was among
6,252 former Umno leaders and members
readmitted under the Rumah Bangsa initiative.
Khairy, the former Rembau member of parliament, also expressed his appreciation to the Umno president and the party's Supreme Council for accepting his application to rejoin the party, saying the decision was made with wisdom and consideration for the party's future.
He said he accepted the decision with humility and gratitude, adding that his time outside the party had given him space for reflection and self-improvement.
"The period outside the party has allowed me to reflect and improve my weaknesses. I return with a sense of responsibility and commitment."
Khairy added that he hopes the decision will mark a new beginning for Umno to move forward in a more united, mature and credible manner.
Umno secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki yesterday confirmed in a statement that a total of 6,252 former Umno leaders and members, including Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein, Khairy Jamaluddin and Tan Sri Syed Hamid Albar, had been readmitted under the Rumah Bangsa initiative.
He said the Supreme Council fully supports and endorses the concept introduced by the party president, which allows the unconditional return of members who were previously suspended, expelled or had left the party for any reason.
Previously, Khairy said he had formally requested that his party membership termination be revoked, with his original membership number reactivated.
He said the appeal letter was submitted under the Rumah Bangsa spirit to the party president during a meeting last month.
In January 2023, he was
sacked from Umno
for allegedly disparaging and sabotaging Barisan Nasional's (BN) campaign during the 15th General Election (GE15).
He was also accused of claiming that Umno had lost its direction and portraying himself as a saviour during the election campaign
A party insider said the Umno Disciplinary Board, in presiding over the then BN candidate for the Sungai Buloh parliamentary seat, had in their investigation several evidences including video recordings of him supposedly disparaged the party.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia stands firm in speaking truth on global stage - Anwar
Article:
KOTA BARU: Malaysia remains firm in its independent and sovereign position to speak the truth at the international level, even if it is difficult for some countries and major world powers to accept, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said this courageous stance is reflected in Malaysia's position on the West Asian conflict, particularly the United States-backed Israeli attacks on Iran.
"In my discussion with the President of Iran (Masoud Pezeshkian), he expressed appreciation to the federal government and the Malaysian Parliament for condemning the attacks by Israel and the United States, and for giving strong support in safeguarding the right to freedom in Iran.
"We extended condolences over the martyrdom of several key religious leaders and Iranian military personnel, as well as schoolchildren.
"Just imagine if this had happened in the West, the whole world would be in uproar for weeks and months condemning the attacks.
"But when it happens to an Islamic country, many remain silent. I was deeply affected when schoolchildren were killed; almost no country issued statements expressing sympathy for the children and their families or condemning these attacks by Israel and the United States," he said when speaking at the opening of the new LTSIP terminal here today.
Also present were Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud, Transport Minister Anthony Loke, and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
On negotiations to resolve the crisis arising from the Israel-US attacks on Iran, Anwar said Malaysia is providing strong support to such efforts through close cooperation with Pakistan, the Gulf states and Turkiye.
However, he warned that any negotiations must not sideline justice by merely demanding Iran's surrender, but must instead take into account the root cause of the tensions, namely the actions of the Zionist regime.
Anwar also expressed gratitude for the strong cross-party solidarity in Malaysia in condemning the injustices, which has received appreciation from the president of Iran.
The prime minister also reminded the public to closely follow developments in the global energy crisis stemming from the West Asian conflict, as the situation is unlikely to be resolved in the near term.
He said that even if peace is achieved, the related economic impact will not recover within just a few months.
"As mentioned by the Emir of Qatar, gas plants and gas companies in Qatar that have been damaged or destroyed will take time to recover.
"They may be able to begin limited operations by the end of the year, but it will take at least one year to reach minimum operations and three years for full operations," he said.
-- Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Postponing Melaka, Johor polls is a personal view
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has clarified that the suggestion to postpone the Melaka and Johor state elections is merely a personal view.
He was responding to a proposal by Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan, who suggested delaying the polls until the current energy crisis subsides.
"That is his personal view and not the stand of Umno or Barisan Nasional," Zahid told reporters after officiating the Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (Kimma) 46th annual general meeting at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur today.
During his speech, Zahid emphasised that the coalition's machinery in Melaka is already in motion.
"Readiness for the machinery to mobilise support from the youth has been underway for some time.
"We know that the members of Barisan Nasional component parties alone are not enough to ensure victory for our candidates, he said.
Zahid said the focus was now on drawing in voters beyond the existing BN membership base.
"I have called for the machinery to start garnering support from voters who are not component party members to join us in supporting our candidates.
"When the state or general elections are held is not the primary concern; what matters is that the groundwork must run from now on, he added.
Yesterday, Zahid said BN must retain its two-thirds majority in Melaka to ensure continuity in the state administration.
He said BN would
leverage
the strong performance of the state government led by Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh as a 'feel-good factor' in facing the Melaka state election, which is expected to be held this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's 'plastic tide' now a threat to health and food security
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's growing "plastic tide" has escalated from an environmental concern into a direct threat to public health and food security, amid rising evidence of microplastic contamination in the food chain.
The Alliance for a Safe Community has called for an urgent overhaul of the country's waste management infrastructure.
Its chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye said findings indicating Malaysians could ingest up to 2,016 microplastic particles annually served as a "grim validation" of long-standing concerns.
"When 93.3 per cent of sampled ikan kembong, a primary protein staple for the rakyat, shows contamination, we are no longer discussing a hypothetical risk," he said in a statement.
"We are discussing a systemic compromise of our national food safety."
Lee also highlighted growing distress among coastal fishing communities, particularly in Perak and Penang, where fishermen had reported up to a 40 per cent drop in catches, alongside increasing sightings of diseased marine life.
He described the situation as a "double blow", with economic and environmental consequences unfolding simultaneously.
"Our fishermen are facing economic displacement as they are forced to travel further offshore to find cleaner waters, while our marine nurseries are being poisoned by plastic debris that acts as a carrier for heavy metals and toxins," he said.
While acknowledging the government's Roadmap Towards Zero Single-Use Plastics 2018–2030, Lee said that current measures might be insufficient to address the growing threat posed by microplastics.
He said that the microscopic nature of the particles, some measuring less than 0.2mm, allows them to enter the human bloodstream, raising concerns over long-term health effects.
"If these particles are small enough to enter the human bloodstream, we cannot afford to wait until 2030.
"The health damage, including endocrine disruption and chronic inflammation, could occur long before the policy reaches its target," he said.
To address what he described as a widening gap between policy and on-the-ground realities, Lee proposed several urgent measures.
These include mandating microplastic filtration systems at municipal wastewater treatment plants, establishing national food safety thresholds for microplastics in seafood, and introducing incentivised "trash-for-cash" programmes to encourage fishermen to collect marine plastic waste.
"We are essentially eating our own waste," he said.
"This is not a fisheries problem; it is a land-based failure showing up on our plates. We must protect the health of our seas to protect the health of our people."
A New Straits Times report today highlighted
growing concerns
in Malaysia's fishing industry, including incidents of dead fish in Sungai Ketil, declining catches in Pengerang, and rising fish prices.
It also cited a recent study indicating that Malaysians could be
ingesting
up to 2,016 microplastic particles annually through fish consumption.
Fishermen have also reported worsening conditions at sea in recent years, with plastic debris becoming increasingly common.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt approves additional RM20 million for poverty eradication in Kelantan
Article:
PASIR PUTEH: The federal government has approved an additional RM20 million allocation for poverty eradication programmes in Kelantan.
The funds will be coordinated by the Kelantan branch of the Prime Minister's Department's Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU).
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the special additional allocation is specifically intended to assist those living below the poverty line.
"I have asked the ICU to manage this," he said during his speech at the Masjid Mukim Gong Chapa Temila here today.
Earlier, Anwar attended a meet-and-greet session at the mosque, which was also attended by Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud.
Anwar said that while poverty eradication programmes were already underway in the state, the menteri besar had requested additional support.
"The menteri besar requested further funding for these programmes. Consequently, I am providing this special additional allocation of RM20 million to help the poor, he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hishammuddin: 'I will continue to serve and fight for Umno'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein has pledged to continue serving the party following the lifting of his suspension and his readmission into Umno.
The Sembrong member of parliament said he remained committed to his responsibilities and principles after the party's Supreme Council decided to lift the suspension.
"Alhamdulillah, the Umno Supreme Council has lifted my suspension after three years.
"I will continue to fight and serve. I will carry out my responsibilities with commitment and principles. Dulu, kini, selamanya (then, now, forever)," he said in a Facebook post today.
Hishammuddin was among 6,252 former Umno leaders and members
readmitted
under the Rumah Bangsa initiative.
Umno secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the Supreme Council fully supported and endorsed the Rumah Bangsa concept introduced by the party president, which allows the unconditional return of members who were previously suspended, expelled or had left the party.
He also said that Hishammuddin had formally
submitted
a request for his suspension to be lifted on March 16.
Asyraf said his office had received several applications from former leaders who were previously suspended or expelled, seeking to rejoin the party.
On March 12, Hishammuddin said he was
ready to return
if his suspension was lifted, stressing that he had never left Umno despite being suspended in January 2023.
"The issue is my status as an Umno member, as the Supreme Council previously suspended me.
"Personally, this is not a big issue as I have never left the party. I was suspended but never attacked the party," he said.
Hishammuddin was suspended from Umno in January 2023 for allegedly breaching party discipline.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia consumes twice its daily oil production – Finance Ministry
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia consumes about 700,000 barrels of oil per day, double its daily production of 350,000 barrels, necessitating continued crude oil imports to meet domestic demand.
The Finance Ministry said today that the conflict in the Middle East has disrupted the global supply chain, including transportation and delivery, while affecting the critical shipping route through the Strait of Hormuz.
"Crude oil and fuel supplies are experiencing disruptions and delays. Crude oil prices have risen by nearly 40 per cent, along with increases in logistics and insurance costs," the ministry said in a statement.
Data from the ministry showed that 48 per cent of the country's petroleum product supply is refined by Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), while the remaining 52 per cent is handled by other domestic oil companies.
While 48 per cent of Malaysia's crude oil is produced locally, the country relies on imports for the remainder.
Of these imports, 38 per cent pass through the Strait of Hormuz, seven per cent are sourced from Southeast Asia and West Africa, and another seven per cent from Middle East and other regions.
The ministry explained that reliance on imports is essential to satisfy domestic demand for petrol, diesel, liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), and jet fuel, as domestic production remains insufficient for national refineries.
"Nearly 40 per cent of the imports pass through the Strait of Hormuz, a major route that is currently affected," it said.
Meanwhile, Petronas confirmed today that the vessel Ocean Thunder has arrived in Malaysia carrying one million barrels of crude oil from Basrah, Iraq.
The shipment is part of the national oil company's ongoing efforts to ensure the stability of the country's fuel supply amid global volatility.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah Health Dept assures patient safety at Queen Elizabeth Hospital
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah State Health Department has assured that patient welfare and safety remain the highest priority in all implementations.
"The hospital guarantees equitable treatment regardless of background, with transparent bed allocation based on clinical needs and availability.
"Queen Elizabeth Hospital's work culture is grounded in professionalism and courteous communication; any breach of ethics will be dealt with firmly in accordance with regulations," it said in a statement.
Recently, a healthcare-focused website, CodeBlue, claimed that regardless of patients' diagnosis or stability, they were being pushed out of the Emergency and Trauma Department (ETD) and placed in a multidisciplinary ward to meet a four-hour ETD clearance target at the hospital.
Since Jan 29 this year, the hospital has implemented the Health Ministry's patient flow management system to reduce congestion at the ETD.
The initiative includes coordination of bed usage through the Bed Management Unit (BMU), the establishment of multidisciplinary wards, and strengthened discharge management through the Expected Date of Discharge (EDD).
A discharge lounge and a bed monitoring system have also been introduced to ensure the availability of space for new patients.
CodeBlue also alleged that the ward was opened without adequate staff or basic equipment to operate safely, as well as lacking basic infection control measures.
It further reported that frontline staff who raised concerns were met with threats such as disciplinary action or punitive transfers.
The article also questioned the allocation of single-bed isolation rooms, alleging that individuals with personal connections to management were given priority, putting severely ill patients at a disadvantage.
"The sustainability of these initiatives will be strengthened through strategic improvements, including regular meetings with stakeholders to ensure open communication, and enhancements to the bed allocation system based on transparent clinical criteria.
"Periodic system evaluations are conducted to improve the effectiveness of patient management in a practical manner while prioritising the well-being of healthcare personnel.
"The Sabah State Health Department appreciates the commitment and sacrifices of healthcare workers who continue to deliver the best services despite heavy workloads.
"All feedback will be carefully reviewed to ensure safe, high-quality and effective healthcare services for the well-being of the people of Sabah," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Digital shift makes unlicensed moneylending syndicates harder to track
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Unlicensed moneylending syndicates, commonly known as 'ah long', are becoming more aggressive and organised, shifting to digital platforms and social media to evade detection.
Federal Commercial Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said the transition from traditional methods to online advertising allowed syndicates to hide their activities while targeting vulnerable groups.
These include low-income individuals, those with poor credit records, or those in financial desperation.
Rusdi said victims faced serious consequences, including exorbitant interest rates, prolonged debt cycles, threats, and harassment.
"In many cases, victims suffer emotional distress, loss of property, and worsening financial problems," he said.
"Police data showed a significant spike in reported cases, which jumped to 2,011 last year from 1,071 in 2024.
"Based on the data, the sharp increase in 2025 shows they are increasingly aggressive in finding victims using digital platforms and social media compared to traditional operations through physical advertisements," Rusdi said.
Over a five-year period from 2021 to 2025, a total of 6,277 cases were recorded nationwide with losses reaching RM28.28 million.
The breakdown recorded 1,000 cases in 2021, 1,009 in 2022, 1,186 in 2023, 1,071 in 2024, and 2,011 in 2025.
Victims lost RM4.02 million in 2021, RM6.96 million in 2022, RM5.93 million in 2023, RM5.30 million in 2024, and RM6.05 million in 2025.
Rusdi said the trend remained worrying, with a continuous upward trajectory.
In response, police have intensified enforcement, conducting 993 raids in 2021, 983 in 2022, 1,111 in 2023, 1,252 in 2024, and 1,631 last year. These operations resulted in 5,849 arrests.
"Although enforcement actions are increasing year by year, syndicates continue to adapt their modus operandi, including the use of online platforms," he said.
Selangor recorded the highest number of cases at 513, followed by Johor (223), Kuala Lumpur (195), Sarawak (167), and Sabah (156).
"Overall, ah long activities remain active and dynamic across states, requiring us to increase operations to prevent more people from becoming victims and to affect their families' well-being," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR denies plan to contest solo in Johor polls
Article:
KOTA BARU: PKR has dismissed claims that it is planning to contest independently in the upcoming Johor state election, saying the reports are inaccurate and misleading.
PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said no discussions or decisions have been made on any move for the party to go solo in the state polls.
She said media reports suggesting otherwise distort the actual political position of the party and the Pakatan Harapan (PH) coalition.
"The political situation in Johor is different from the federal level. At the national level, the government is formed through cooperation between PH and other parties under the Unity government.
"However, in Johor, the state government is led by Barisan Nasional, making PH the opposition bloc.
"In Johor, PH is not part of the state government. We do not have representation in local councils, village leadership or state agencies, which clearly reflects our position as the opposition," she said at a press conference after the Sentuhan Kasih and Rahmah Madani Sales Programme here today.
Fuziah, who is also deputy domestic trade and cost of living minister, said PH must be prepared for all possibilities ahead of the next state election, including contesting independently if circumstances require it.
"If needed, PH can stand alone as a coalition, but that does not mean PKR will contest alone, as we remain committed to our partners in PH."
She added that the priority remains strengthening cooperation within the coalition, while acknowledging the political realities in Johor.
"I admit the current situation in Johor requires PH to be ready to contest as the opposition. We must be prepared for any scenario.
"This is not just about PKR, but PH as a coalition."
An online portal previously reported that PKR was planning to contest independently in the Johor state election, which is expected to be held later this year.
On March 5, Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa was reported to have said the party had identified 28 potential seats to contest, which had been discussed and agreed by the state leadership council.
Separately, Johor Amanah chairman Aminolhuda Hassan said the party had identified 20 state seats it intends to contest.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Apad issues show-cause letter to bus company over dangerous driving video
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Land Public Transport Agency (Apad) has issued a show-cause letter to Billion Stars Express Sdn Bhd after a viral video surfaced showing one of its express bus drivers driving dangerously.
In a statement today, Apad said it viewed the incident seriously as it could endanger passengers and other road users.
Investigations are being carried out on the bus, registration number BSB 4999, under the Land Public Transport Act 2010 (Act 715).
The probe includes potential violations under Section 22(1)(b)(iii) regarding the conduct of public service vehicle drivers and Section 23(1)(b) concerning the failure of operators to prioritise safety.
Apad said the company is also being investigated under Clause 7 of the General Conditions of Service Level Requirements, which mandates that licensed operators ensure their employees maintain proper conduct while on duty.
The show-cause letter was issued on April 16 under Section 27 of Act 715, which empowers the agency to act against licence holders who breach operating conditions.
The public can lodge complaints regarding public transport misconduct via the Transport Ministry's hotline at 1-800-88-7723 or through WhatsApp at +6019-290 7723.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two held with 5kg cannabis worth nearly RM400,000
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: Two men, including a foreigner, were detained for possession of 5kg of cannabis buds worth nearly RM400,000 here this morning.
The two were detained by General Operations Force (GOF) personnel at a roadblock in Serongga.
GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the suspects — a local man and a Sri Lankan — were travelling in a Perodua Alza when they were arrested.
"The two were stopped by GOF personnel from the Ninth Battalion at the Serongga roadblock at about 10.30am.
"During further inspection of the car, they found a black bag containing five compressed blocks believed to be cannabis buds.
"The estimated value of the drugs is about RM80,000 per kg, bringing the total value of the 5kg to nearly RM400,000," he said.
He said the foreigner, in his 50s, was found to have an expired social visit pass.
"He and the local man, in his 30s, were then brought to our base in Taman Bakti for further checks," he said.
He added that the case is being investigated under the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six Perlis Pas assemblymen demand explanation over sudden state assembly postponement
Article:
PADANG BESAR: Six Pas assemblymen are demanding an explanation from Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah over the sudden postponement of the state legislative assembly sitting without a valid reason.
The sitting was scheduled for April 21 to 23, but state assembly secretary Mohd Anwar Sahul Hameed announced the postponement in an official letter yesterday without providing new dates.
Former menteri besar and Sanglang assemblyman Mohd Shukri Ramli described the move as extraordinary, noting it was made in the absence of emergencies such as natural disasters, deaths, or disease outbreaks.
He said the original notice was received on April 9, but the cancellation first came to light via a viral letter before official notification arrived yesterday.
"I received an offer to perform the haj with a flight scheduled for April 18. Because of the assembly sitting, I requested Tabung Haji to postpone my flight to May 3.
"Suddenly, the assembly is postponed. This is deeply regrettable," he told a press conference at the Mata Ayer constituency service centre today.
Shukri said the sitting was crucial to address outstanding issues, including investment progress, Temporary Occupation Licence (TOL) land grant management, and the approval of the Padang Besar local authority.
He also raised concerns regarding the simultaneous transfer of several heads of department without immediate replacements, which he claimed could stifle administration and dampen investor confidence.
Representing the six Pas assemblymen, Shukri expressed disappointment that they were not consulted regarding the postponement.
"The unity of the six Pas assemblymen is the strength of the state government. We should have been consulted as we are the pillars supporting the government today.
"As the head of government, the menteri besar cannot maintain a sense of distrust toward us; he must be confident in our support," he said.
The group has given Abu Bakar one week to provide an official explanation to end negative perceptions and restore public confidence in the state administration's harmony.
Commenting on the current political cooperation between Pas and Bersatu in Perlis, Shukri said the relationship remains intact under the Perikatan Nasional (PN) banner.
The sitting would have been the first since the Perlis PN political crisis in December, which saw Shukri resign as menteri besar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MIC still in BN despite letter to PN, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MIC remains a core component party of Barisan Nasional (BN), BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi confirmed.
Asked by reporters about MIC's status within the coalition, he was brief and unambiguous.
"As of now, they are members or a core party in Barisan Nasional.
"They have not stated they are leaving Barisan Nasional officially," he said, speaking to reporters after opening Kimma's 46th annual general meeting (AGM) at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur here today.
Zahid, who is also Umno president, said that MIC had since denied ever having applied to join the opposition coalition, despite the existence of a letter to Perikatan Nasional (PN).
"Although there was a letter sent to PN, they denied that they had ever applied to join the party concerned despite a letter showing so," he said.
On March 25, MIC deputy president Datuk Seri M. Saravanan said the party had decided to maintain the status quo and
remain with BN
.
He clarified that the letter in question was not an application but rather an enquiry following an invitation extended by Pas leaders, including its deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man.
He said that when the decision to explore a potential coalition with PN was made during the party's AGM last year, Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin was still the PN chairman.
However, with Pas now leading the opposition coalition following the appointment of its vice-president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as PN chairman, Saravanan said MIC needs to check the pulse of the Indian community before making any decision.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pakistan's military chief concludes three-day visit to Iran
Article:
ISLAMABAD, (Pakistan): Pakistan's powerful army chief completed a three-day visit to Teheran, the country's military said on Saturday, having met top Iranian leaders and negotiators as part of efforts to end the Middle East war.
Field Marshal Asim Munir met Iran's president, foreign minister, parliament speaker and the head of Iran's military central command centre, the statement said.
The visit showed Pakistan's "unwavering resolve to facilitate a negotiated settlement... and to promote peace, stability, and prosperity", the military said ahead of expected US-Iran talks in Islamabad in the coming days.
Parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi led the Iranian delegation to Islamabad for peace talks with the United States last week, the highest level face-to-face contact between the two countries in decades.
Those talks ended without an agreement, but diplomacy continued thereafter, with Pakistan's prime minister undertaking a three-country tour to Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkey to push the peace process.
A second round of talks is expected in Islamabad this coming week.
In Teheran, "the Field Marshal underscored the need for dialogue, de-escalation, and peaceful resolution of outstanding issues through sustained diplomatic engagements", the Pakistani statement said.
Teheran threatened on Saturday to shut the Strait of Hormuz once more if the United States continued its blockade of Iranian ports, hours after Iran announced it had reopened the strategic waterway in the wake of a ceasefire in Lebanon. — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kimma's BN bid lacks unanimous backing, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kongres Indian Muslim Malaysia's (Kimma) bid to become a core component party of Barisan Nasional (BN) remains stalled by a constitutional requirement that demands unanimous agreement from all existing component parties, and that agreement has yet to materialise.
BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi called out component parties that had allegedly signalled no objection to Kimma's entry but had yet to back that position in writing.
"The BN Constitution has not yet been amended because it specifies that 100 per cent of BN leadership, consisting of core party leaders, must approve any amendment to accept a new party.
"And so far, there has been no agreement for that amendment," he said in his speech officiating Kimma's 46th Annual General Meeting (AGM) at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur here today.
Present were Kimma president Datuk Seri Syed Ibrahim Kader and BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir.
Zahid, who is also Umno president, said if a party truly had no objection to Kimma's membership, it should prove it at the next BN Supreme Council meeting with an official written commitment to amend the constitution, not only to admit Kimma, but also to open the door to other aspiring parties.
Without naming any party or individual, Zahid took a pointed swipe at those whose public and private positions did not align.
"I don't want different media statements, different speeches and different talk in meetings.
"We must sound loyal behind the scenes, the same as in public — that is what's called being loyal," he said.
He was equally firm in affirming Kimma's standing within the coalition, saying neither Umno nor BN had ever doubted the loyalty of the organisation, which has long been a partner via Friends of BN.
"Loyalty has its price, and that price must be paid.
"No matter how expensive the price of loyalty is, it must be paid," he said.
Earlier, Syed Ibrahim laid bare the organisation's frustration at being excluded from becoming a BN component party.
The president also highlighted that recent public statements from a component party, previously regarded as the main obstacle to Kimma's entry, had signalled a change in position, with that party stating that it no longer stood in the way of any party seeking to join BN.
"So we are left wondering; what further reasons are there to delay our entry?
"For 50 years we have stood behind this struggle without a shadow of doubt," he said.
Kimma is currently within the Friends of BN group of affiliate members to the coalition, which includes the Indian Progressive Front (IPF), Parti Cinta Malaysia, the Malaysian Makkal Sakti Party and Parti Punjabi Malaysia (PPM).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 5.0-magnitude quake hits Nagano Prefecture in central Japan
Article:
TOKYO: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 5.0 struck the northern part of Nagano Prefecture in central Japan today, the country's weather agency said.
According to Kyodo News Agency, the 1.20pm quake registered an upper 5 on Japan's seven-level seismic intensity scale in Omachi and occurred at a depth of 10 kilometres, according to the Japan Meteorological Agency.
An upper 5 is defined by the agency as a level at which many people find it difficult to walk.
There were no immediate reports of injuries or property damage from the quake in the landlocked prefecture. – BERNAMA-KYODO
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump teases 'good news' on Iran as ceasefire clock ticks down
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said there had been "some pretty good news" about Iran, suggesting optimism about peace talks to end the war in the Middle East, but he said a ceasefire in the conflict may not be extended without a deal by Wednesday.
Iran hours earlier had temporarily reopened the Strait of Hormuz following a separate US-brokered ceasefire agreement by Israel and Lebanon. But subsequent statements from both sides left uncertainty over how quickly shipping might return to normal through the global oil choke point.
Trump, speaking on Air Force One late on Friday, declined to elaborate on what the good news was.
UNCLEAR IF ANY DIRECT TALKS THIS WEEKEND
"It seems to be going very well in the Middle East with Iran," he told reporters while returning to Washington from Phoenix, Arizona. "We're negotiating over the weekend. I expect things to go well. Many of these things have been negotiated and agreed to.
"The main thing is that Iran will not have a nuclear weapon. You cannot let Iran have a nuclear weapon, and that supersedes everything else."
But in sharp contrast, he said he may end the ceasefire with Iran unless a long-term deal to end the war is agreed before it expires on Wednesday, adding that a US blockade of Iranian ports would continue.
The war with Iran, which began on Feb 28 with a US-Israeli attack, has killed thousands and sent oil prices surging because of the de facto closure of the strait, which until recently carried about a fifth of the world's oil trade.
Trump has told Reuters there would probably be more direct talks between Iran and the US this weekend. Some diplomats said that was unlikely given the logistics of gathering in Islamabad, where the talks are expected to take place.
There were no signs of preparations early on Saturday for talks in the Pakistani capital, where the highest-level US-Iran negotiations since the 1979 Islamic Revolution ended without agreement last weekend.
The key Pakistani mediator, army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir, has been holding talks in Teheran since Wednesday.
A Pakistani source aware of mediation efforts said a meeting between Iran and the US could produce an initial memorandum of understanding, followed by a comprehensive peace agreement within 60 days.
Among complicating factors, Iran's speaker of parliament and senior negotiator, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, posted on social media that the Strait of Hormuz "will not remain open" if the US blockade continued.
Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi had announced on social media that the strait was open for all commercial vessels for the remainder of the 10-day truce that was agreed on Thursday by Israel and Lebanon, which was invaded by Israel after the Iran-allied Hizbollah group joined the fighting.
Vessel traffic data showed a group of around 20 ships, including container ships, bulk carriers and tankers, moving through the Gulf toward the Strait of Hormuz on Friday evening, but most ended up turning back, although it was not clear why. The group included three container ships operated by French shipping group CMA CGM, which declined to comment.
A cruise ship that was stranded in Dubai, the Celestyal Discovery, however crossed the strait and was headed to Oman on Saturday morning, according to Marine Traffic vessel tracking data.
Iran has said all ships crossing the strait must coordinate with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, which was not the case before the war. The Defence Ministry said in a statement quoted by state television that military vessels and ships linked to "hostile forces" - the US and Israel - were still not permitted to pass.
Shipping companies have said they would require clarifications, including about the risk of mines, before vessels moved through the entry point to the Gulf.
The US Navy warned seafarers that the mine threat in parts of the waterway was not fully understood and said they should consider avoiding the area.
NO CLARITY ON IRAN'S NUCLEAR PROGRAM
It also was unclear how Iran and the US would address Teheran's nuclear program, which has been a key sticking point in peace talks, with Iran defending its right to what it says is a civilian nuclear energy program.
Trump told Reuters the US would remove Iran's stockpiles of enriched uranium. Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei told state TV the material would not be transferred anywhere.
Separately, a senior Iranian official said Teheran hoped a preliminary agreement could be reached in the coming days.
Oil prices  fell about 10pct and global stocks jumped on Friday on the prospect of marine traffic resuming through the strait.
After a video conference on Friday, more than a dozen countries said they were willing to join an international mission to protect shipping in the strait when conditions permit, Britain said.
A senior Iranian official told Reuters there had been an agreement on unfreezing billions of dollars in Iranian assets as part of the accord, without giving a timeline. Later on Friday, Trump, who has repeatedly referred to a peace agreement as a "deal" or "transaction," told a rally in Arizona that "no money will exchange hands in any way, shape or form."
At last weekend's talks, the US proposed a 20-year suspension of all Iranian nuclear activity, while Iran suggested a halt of three to five years, according to people familiar with the proposals.
Two Iranian sources have said there were signs of a compromise that could remove part of the stockpile.
Trump told Reuters the US might not act quickly. "We're going to go in with Iran, at a nice leisurely pace, and go down and start excavating with big machinery," he said in a phone interview. "We'll bring it back to the United States."
Despite Trump's optimism, Iranian sources told Reuters that "gaps remained to be resolved" before a preliminary agreement, while senior clerics struck a defiant tone during Friday prayers.
"Our people do not negotiate while being humiliated," cleric Ahmad Khatami said.--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan, Australia seal frigate development deal
Article:
MELBOURNE: Japan and Australia said Saturday they have concluded a contract to jointly develop a new fleet for the Australian Navy based on the upgraded Japanese Mogami-class frigate, Kyodo News reported.
The confirmation came when Japanese Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi and his Australian counterpart Richard Marles held talks in Melbourne.
Koizumi said during the meeting that he hopes to take Japan's relations with Australia to new heights.
The vessels are expected to be initially built in Japan, effectively making the deal a de facto export of Japanese-designed warships.
Japan's rules for defence equipment transfers place strict limits on the export of weapons with lethal capabilities but allow such exports when they are part of joint development and production projects.
Japan views Australia as a "quasi-ally" and a key partner in maintaining stability in the Indo-Pacific. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nearly 6,000 foreign vehicles fined RM2.19mil in VEP crackdown
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has issued 5,984 summonses totalling RM2.19 million since the enforcement of the Vehicle Entry Permit (VEP) at the Malaysia-Singapore land border began on July 1 last year.
RTD director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said 82,987 Singaporean vehicles were inspected during this period near the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB) Customs, Immigration, and Quarantine (CIQ) complexes, as well as in Taman Daya.
"Summonses were issued to owners or drivers of private and company vehicles that failed to register for a VEP, held expired permits, or were still under pre-registration status.
"Offenders must settle their summonses and register for the VEP before leaving Malaysia, in accordance with Section 66J of the Road Transport Act 1987," he said.
Aedy Fadly spoke at a press conference following a joint VEP enforcement operation with the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry at the BSI CIQ exit last night.
Also present were the ministry's director-general Datuk Azman Adam and Johor RTD director Zulkarnain Yasin.
Providing a breakdown of registrations, Aedy Fadly said 391,007 VEP Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tags had been issued as of last Thursday.
Of these, 322,048 tags for individual private vehicles are active, while 14,771 remain inactive. For company-owned vehicles, 47,496 tags are active while 7,792 are inactive.
He said full VEP enforcement is aimed at ensuring high compliance among foreign-registered vehicles while curbing the leakage of national fuel subsidies, particularly RON95 petrol.
"This strategic cooperation involves integrating the VEP system with Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) technology for real-time tracking of foreign vehicles.
"This allows agencies to share integrated data, including owner profiles and travel records, enhancing enforcement efficiency and the ability to detect subsidy abuse," he said.
He added that integrated operations between the RTD and the ministry would be intensified and eventually expanded to the country's northern borders.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Caltex Malaysia announces temporary fuel supply disruption in five states due to port delay
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Caltex Malaysia has announced that several of its service stations across five states may experience temporary disruptions in the supply of RON95 petrol and diesel over the coming days, beginning yesterday.
In an Instagram post, Caltex stated that the affected states are Perlis, Kedah, Penang, Perak, and Kelantan.
The company explained that the disruption is due to an unexpected port delay, which has impacted its scheduled fuel deliveries.
It added that efforts are underway to prioritise distribution and minimise downtime.
Caltex also apologised for any inconvenience caused and thanked customers for their patience and understanding. — Bernama

Title: What you need to know: Why Malaysia still imports oil and how the Hormuz disruption is driving up costs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Malaysia produces crude oil, but it still relies heavily on imports to meet domestic fuel demand that is nearly double local output, while global supply disruptions are also pushing up costs across the oil supply chain.
The situation reflects both structural supply gaps at home and rising volatility in international oil markets following tensions in West Asia, the Finance Ministry explained today.
Malaysia continues to operate a “sell high, buy low” strategy, exporting higher-value crude while importing other grades of oil to ensure sufficient supply for domestic consumption.
This allows the country to maximise revenue from exports while keeping enough crude available for refining petrol, diesel, liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) and jet fuel locally.
Why does Malaysia still import oil despite being a producer?
Malaysia’s domestic oil consumption stands at about 700,000 barrels per day, nearly double its production of around 350,000 barrels per day.
This imbalance means the country must import roughly half of its crude oil needs to keep fuel supply stable.
Even though Malaysia produces oil, local output alone is not enough to meet demand across transport, industry and aviation sectors.
The ‘sell high, buy low’ strategy
Malaysia exports premium crude oil that fetches higher prices globally while importing other crude grades that are more cost-effective for domestic refining.
The Finance Ministry said 48 per cent of petroleum products are refined by Petronas,  while the rest is processed by other oil companies operating in the country.
This structure supports both export earnings and domestic fuel availability under a single integrated supply system.
The Strait of Hormuz link
The Finance Ministry said the conflict in West Asia has disrupted global oil supply chains, including transport routes and delivery timelines.
A key concern is the Strait of Hormuz, through which about 20 per cent of global oil trade flows. This includes nearly 40 per cent of Malaysia’s crude imports.
Disruptions in the waterway have contributed to delays in shipments and higher risk premiums for shipping and insurance.
Crude oil prices have risen by almost 40 per cent, with additional increases in logistics and insurance costs further raising overall supply chain expenses.
When demand is higher than production
Malaysia sources about 48 per cent of its crude oil domestically, while 38 per cent is imported via the Strait of Hormuz, with the rest coming from South-east Asia, West Africa and other regions.
But most crude oil used in local refineries still needs to be imported because domestic production cannot fully meet national demand.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has previously said Petronas is now a net importer of fuel, reflecting Malaysia’s shifting energy balance.
Petronas confirmed the arrival of its tanker Ocean Thunder this morning with its cargo of one million barrels of crude oil from Basrah, Iraq, as part of efforts to stabilise fuel supply.
Despite being an oil-producing country, Malaysia’s reliance on imports – combined with global disruptions like those affecting the Strait of Hormuz – means both supply security and costs are increasingly tied to international developments.

Title: Malaysia link emerges in sexual abuse and conversion scandal at global IT giant TCS in India
Article:
NASHIK (India), April 18 — Authorities in India have uncovered a Malaysia-based link in a scandal involving allegations of sexual harassment and forced religious conversion at global IT services giant Tata Consultancy Services (TCS).
The man has been identified as “Imran” and described as a purported preacher believed to be based in Malaysia, who allegedly communicated with victims through online platforms, India-based news outlets
News18
and
First Post
reported earlier this week.
The allegations purport to be systematic abuse at one of the TCS facilities in Nashik, a city in the western state of Maharashtra.
Digital evidence from WhatsApp chats and video calls linked “Imran” to the wider network are currently under investigation.
Indian police said the investigation began after a covert operation in which female officers posed as cleaning staff to gather evidence inside the premises.
So far, nine First Information Reports (FIR) have been filed by eight women and one man, involving allegations of sexual harassment, rape under false promises of marriage, and coercion to convert religion.
A FIR is the official police complaint in India that formally starts a criminal investigation into a reported offence.
The Indian investigators said at least 12 employees were identified as potential targets over a period of several years.
They are also analysing encrypted chats that allegedly show coordination among staff to identify and approach vulnerable colleagues.
The victims were allegedly offered promotions and overseas job opportunities as part of the recruitment and influence tactics.
Six suspects, including team leaders, have been arrested, while others remain under investigation.
TCS, one of India’s biggest private employers, has responded to these allegations, saying it enforces a strict zero-tolerance policy and has suspended employees named in the probe.
Indian authorities are now working to verify the identity and role of the Malaysia-based individual and determine whether the case forms part of a wider cross-border network.

Title: PM: 85pc of Malaysians paying subsidised diesel rates, including fishermen, school bus services
Article:
KOTA BHARU, April 18 — Eighty-five per cent of Malaysians are unaffected by market diesel prices because they still enjoy the government’s subsidised rates, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
The Prime Minister said only 15 per cent of the people are affected by the fuel price increase due to the current global energy crisis.
“I did a study yesterday. The high diesel price does have an impact. On whom? Fifteen per cent of the people. Eighty-five per cent of the people are not using diesel at the market price; they use subsidised prices.
“But what is being highlighted is the 15 per cent that are affected. The 85 per cent are not mentioned at all,” he said during the opening ceremony of the new terminal at Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) here today.
Also present were Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud, Transport Minister Anthony Loke, and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, explained that for the transport sector, the government provides exemptions for school buses and certain strategic transport lorries.
He also raised an issue discussed at a state legislative assembly regarding claims that fishermen are affected by the rise in market diesel prices.
“Fishermen use subsidised diesel prices, not market diesel prices. So do not give that example,” he emphasised.
Separately, the Ministry of Finance provided an update on current fuel prices, confirming that subsidised RON95 under the BUDI Madani (BUDI95) programme remains at RM1.99 per litre for the period of April 16 to 22.
For unsubsidised retail prices, RON95 and RON97 have each dropped by 25 sen per litre to RM4.02 and RM5.10 respectively, while unsubsidised diesel has dropped by 75 sen to RM5.97 per litre for the same period. — Bernama

Title: PM Anwar says Petronas negotiating with Russia for oil is an option, domestic needs priority over supplying other nations
Article:
KOTA BHARU, April 18 — Petroliam Nasional Berhad (Petronas) can negotiate with Russia for oil supply as a proactive measure to ensure the country’s energy reserves remain sufficient for public consumption, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said this step can be taken as many European countries, which along with the United States previously imposed various sanctions on Moscow, are now again competing to secure oil supplies from Russia due to current economic needs.
“Alhamdulillah, our relations with Russia remain good. So, in the current situation, our team, including Petronas, can negotiate with Russia to meet part of our needs as a friendly nation,” he said when speaking at the official opening of the new terminal of LTSIP here today.
Also present were Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud, Transport Minister Anthony Loke and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
Anwar said geopolitical tensions involving Iran, the United States and Europe have a direct impact on the global transport sector as oil prices and fertiliser supplies are affected.
“Early diplomatic action by the government has also ensured that the country’s oil tankers were among the earliest to pass through critical routes in the Strait of Hormuz, thereby avoiding major disruptions to the national energy supply chain,” he said.
He added that a Petronas oil tanker safely arrived at the Pengerang Integrated Complex in Johor yesterday, and the arrival of the supply is crucial as oil refining processes can only be carried out at that facility.
He said the success was the result of early engagement by the government with the Iranian leadership, which enabled passage to be granted while international negotiations over the future of the Strait of Hormuz remained deadlocked.
“The global crisis has also led other countries to seek Malaysia’s cooperation, including Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, who recently visited the country to discuss supplies of petrol, diesel and fertiliser,” he said.
However, Anwar stressed that the government’s position remains to prioritise the interests of the people and the domestic market before considering any assistance to other countries.
“Domestic needs are given priority. If there is a surplus, we help friendly nations, but if not, we focus on our own people first,” he stressed. — Bernama

Title: BN to refine seat formula but stick to existing framework, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today urged party machinery to begin groundwork immediately for upcoming elections, saying timing is secondary to preparedness on the ground.
“Whether state or general elections are held sooner or later is not the issue. What matters is that the groundwork must start now,” he said.
“We cannot rely on party members alone to win. We must go out and reach voters beyond our base.”
Speaking after attending the Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (KIMMA) 46th annual general meeting at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur, Zahid said early mobilisation is key as BN prepares for state polls and the next general election.
He also addressed calls by Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan to postpone elections in Johor and Melaka due to economic concerns, distancing the coalition from the suggestion.
“That is his personal opinion and not the position of Umno or Barisan Nasional,” he said, making clear that election plans remain on track.
On seat allocations, Zahid said BN will largely stick to its existing formula, while allowing room for adjustments involving component parties and allies.
“I am confident that in the upcoming state elections and particularly the general election, we will use the same formula,” he said.
“We may refine or increase the number of seats where necessary.”
He added that parties such as KIMMA and other “Friends of BN” have previously been accommodated under the arrangement, reflecting the coalition’s long-standing cooperation.
“Allocations have been given before, and we will look at how to adjust based on current needs and realities,” he said.

Title: PM Anwar: Malaysia stands firm in speaking truth on global stage
Article:
KOTA BHARU, April 18 — Malaysia remains firm in its independent and sovereign position to speak the truth at the international level, even if it is difficult for some countries and major world powers to accept, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
The Prime Minister said this courageous stance is reflected in Malaysia’s position on the West Asian conflict, particularly the United States-backed Israeli attacks on Iran.
"In my discussion with the President of Iran (Masoud Pezeshkian), he expressed appreciation to the federal government and the Malaysian Parliament for condemning the attacks by Israel and the United States, and for giving strong support in safeguarding the right to freedom in Iran.
"We extended condolences over the martyrdom of several key religious leaders and Iranian military personnel, as well as schoolchildren. Just imagine if this had happened in the West, the whole world would be in uproar for weeks and months condemning the attacks.
"But when it happens to an Islamic country, many remain silent. I was deeply affected when schoolchildren were killed; almost no country issued statements expressing sympathy for the children and their families or condemning these attacks by Israel and the United States,” he said when speaking at the opening of the new LTSIP terminal here today.
Also present were Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud, Transport Minister Anthony Loke, and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.

Title: Man jailed for using fake payslip to buy products on instalment
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, April 18 — The Magistrate’s Court here sentenced a 37-year-old man, who used a forged payslip as genuine to purchase electrical items on instalment, to nine months in jail.
Magistrate Wan Farrah Farriza Wan Ghazali meted out the sentence to Nikolaus Owin Yohanes Laba after he pleaded guilty to dishonestly using a payslip in his name purportedly issued by an auto accessories company, knowing it to be false, on July 3, 2023, at 12.54pm, at Kwang Woon Trading Sdn Bhd in Taman Indah Permai, Manggatal.
The offence under Section 471 of the Penal Code, punishable under Section 465 of the same Code, carries a jail term of up to two years or fine, or both, on conviction.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Akaash Singh informed the court that Nikolaus had purchased a television and an air-conditioner on instalment from a shop, using the payslip as supporting documentation.
The items were delivered to him on the same day.
However, the following day, the company was alerted that the payslip and employment details were suspicious.
Checks revealed that Nikolaus had never been employed by the company stated in the document.
Attempts to recover the items failed as Nikolaus could not be contacted, resulting in losses amounting to RM6,568.
Investigations further revealed that Nikolaus had submitted a payslip in his own name purportedly issued by the said auto accessories company, despite never having been employed there, to facilitate the instalment purchase of the television and air-conditioner.
In another case, a 32-year-old man was jailed eight months for assisting to conceal stolen property.
Jasmin Suaib pleaded guilty before Magistrate Marilyn Kelvin to intentionally assisting to conceal a stolen Proton Saga tyre with a 14-inch rim at a roadside bush area in Kampung Likas, here, on April 5, this year, at 2pm.
The offence under Section 414 of the Penal Code carries a jail term of between six months and seven months and liability of a fine, on conviction.
The court heard Inspector Yusdi Basri, who prosecuted, that the item had earlier been reported missing from a locked store at a house in Kampung Muhibbah, Likas, on March 28.
Acting on information, police arrested Jasmin later that night and recovered the stolen item hidden in a bush.
Investigations established that Jasmin had knowingly concealed the stolen property. — Daily Express

Title: Man fined RM48,000 for possessing carcasses, live specimen of various protected species
Article:
SANDAKAN, April 18 —  A 38-year-old was fined RM48,000 after pleading guilty to a charge of possessing animals from protected species at the Sessions Court here on Thursday.
Hendry Rayner Endim, a plantation officer, pleaded guilty after the charge was read before Sessions Court Judge Roslinda Razali.
According to the charge sheet, the accused was found in possession of three carcasses of short-tailed pythons (blood pythons), one reticulated python, one carcass of a Malay civet, and two carcasses of common palm civets.
These animals are classified as protected species under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997.
The offence was alleged to have been committed at about 2.10pm on May 24, 2025, along the main Paitan–Kanibongan road, without any valid permit under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997.
The accused was charged under Section 41(2) of the same enactment, which provides for a fine of not less than RM30,000 and not more than RM100,000, or imprisonment for not less than six months and not more than five years, or both.
